---
title: "Exercise 4: Regression I"
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
Describe how regression models are implemented in Python.

- Implement and interpret a regression model in Python using standard libraries.
- Evaluate model performance using appropriate regression metrics.
- Explain how regression modeling fits into the CRISP-DM process, including deployment of trained models.
:::

::: callout-note
- start with one simple example

TBD: include exploration of a dataset on kaggle
follow CRISP-DM to design notebooks (groups, present to the class)
At the end: use joblib to save and load models (to make a prediction) - illustrating the deployment phase of CRISP-DM

- see /home/gerit/workspace/projects/bda/nextcloud/Material/business-analytics-fau/4_Regression.ipynb (plus solutions)
- https://github.com/krishnaik06/Multiple-Linear-Regression/tree/master

https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/15-071-the-analytics-edge-spring-2017/pages/linear-regression/assignment-2/
:::

## Exam prep

TODO : include specific questions/tasks that could be included in the exam.
